behaviors.description: Validate required patient fields before any downstream steps; return one clear error listing all missing fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between validation and downstream pipeline stages (MOCK-MISS yes). No real OS/network/model dependency. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is only about pre-gate validation and error reporting, not real infra or model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with missing `name` and `insurance_id`; assert response contains both missing fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date-of-birth validation rejects future dates, >150-year-old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure validation/parsing logic in one boundary; no module interaction required to expose real failure (MOCK-MISS no). No real infra or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB parser/validator with valid edge dates, future date, 151-years-ago date, and malformed strings.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code and maps `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending` correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is adapter wiring: request construction + response mapping across service/client boundary (MOCK-MISS yes). Rejected System: behavior does not require real network conditions, only correctness of payload and mapping. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decision chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub payer endpoint/client to capture outbound payload and return each status; assert exact sent fields and internal status mapping.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, mark eligibility as `pending` and continue intake instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is cross-module fallback behavior (insurance error -> pipeline continuation). Network outage can be represented by injected timeout/connection error without real OS/network (REAL-INFRA not required here). Rejected System because behavior text says only 'unreachable' fallback semantics, not transport-level behavior under real network.
behaviors.test_description: Stub payer client to throw connection/timeout error; assert eligibility becomes `pending` and triage/scheduling/confirmation still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This tests LLM-call wrapper/input assembly only. Per deterministic-assertion rule, infra around the call can be tested with stubs. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is payload-content inclusion, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms and rubric config against a stub model client; assert outbound request contains both fields exactly.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment/output quality (LLM-DEP yes; AI-CALL-DEFAULT applies). Mocked model would hide this failure (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow: single agentic decision, not multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on curated emergency symptom cases; assert urgency is high with pass-rate threshold and capture drift/failure examples.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser persists a clinician-readable explanation field, not just numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser-to-storage handoff (multiple modules). No need for real model; deterministic triage fixture can expose missing-field persistence. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about storing parsed field, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser a deterministic triage response containing score + explanation; persist via repository and assert stored record includes non-empty explanation column/text.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation references case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality evaluation of real AI text (LLM-DEP yes). Stubbed responses would always pass and hide failure (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow because it evaluates one model output step.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on diverse symptom cases; score explanation for mention of case symptoms and explicit urgency rationale using rubric/heuristics; fail on generic templated responses.

behaviors.description: Scheduler picks first available slot matching urgency SLA: high same-day, medium within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Requires interaction of urgency input + slot-query logic + calendar persistence. No real OS/network/model dependency. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic scheduling policy.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with controlled availability; run scheduler for high and medium urgency; assert selected slot is earliest valid one meeting time window constraints.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is transactional/concurrency interaction across scheduler and shared DB; requires concurrent execution against real local DB semantics (not single-function unit). Rejected System because no external OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated beyond local DB concurrency.
behaviors.test_description: Start two concurrent intake/scheduling requests targeting same candidate slot against test DB with real constraints/transactions; assert only one booking succeeds and second is re-routed/fails gracefully.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Can be caught at message rendering/composition function boundary with deterministic inputs; no required cross-module/infra/model dependency. Rejected Integration+: behavior text is content composition requirements only.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter with fixture appointment/patient/visit-type inputs; assert required fields appear and prep instructions match visit type mapping.

behaviors.description: Deterministic pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to orchestration with deterministic stubbed triage, so model-quality dependence is disqualified by text. Needs module handoff/state propagation checks across pipeline. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due explicit 'stubbed triage output'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end intake in-process with stub triage and stub payer responses plus local test DB; assert each stage runs in order and final confirmation reflects staged outputs.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a full live agent pipeline with real AI triage plus external insurance and end-to-end stage sequencing (MULTI-STEP + AI-CALL-DEFAULT). Higher-level failures emerge from cross-stage interactions under live dependencies. Rejected lower levels because behavior text explicitly requires 'full live intake pipeline' and 'real patient submission'.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end submission using live payer API credentials/sandbox and live triage model, then verify automated completion through scheduling and confirmation with no operator action.
